items.title: AMOS Stealer delivered via Cursor AI agent session
items.description: On April 23, 2026, Field Effect MDR identified AMOS Stealer malware delivered through a novel technique exploiting Cursor AI agent sessions running Claude Code. The attack employed social engineering to manipulate operators into prompting the AI agent to download and execute malicious AppleScript loaders. The heavily obfuscated scripts performed sandbox evasion checks, collected sensitive data inc
items.ai_summary: The AMOS Stealer malware is delivered through a novel technique exploiting Cursor AI agent sessions running Claude Code, leveraging social engineering to manipulate operators into prompting the AI agent to download and execute malicious AppleScript loaders. The attack employs heavily obfuscated scripts that perform sandbox evasion checks and collect sensitive data, with TTPs detected including T1059.002 for AppleScript execution, T1204 for user execution, and T1027 for obfuscated files or information. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement strict controls on AI agent sessions, ensuring that only authorized scripts are executed, and monitor for suspicious AppleScript activity. Additionally, defenders should update their defenses to detect and prevent T1497 virtualization/sandbox evasion and T1562 impairment of defenses techniques. Specific remediation steps include configuring AI agent sessions to only allow execution of trusted scripts and implementing robust monitoring and logging to detect potential security incidents.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1027| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1497| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: The npm Threat Landscape: Attack Surface and Mitigations
items.description: The npm ecosystem experienced a critical shift in September 2025 with the Shai-Hulud worm, marking the transition from isolated attacks to systematic supply chain compromises. In April 2026, TeamPCP launched a coordinated campaign through a malicious @bitwarden/cli package targeting multiple distribution channels including Docker Hub, GitHub Actions, and VS Code extensions. The multi-stage payload
items.ai_summary: The npm ecosystem has been targeted by a coordinated campaign launched by TeamPCP in April 2026, utilizing a malicious @bitwarden/cli package to compromise multiple distribution channels, including Docker Hub, GitHub Actions, and VS Code extensions. This multi-stage payload attack exploits the supply chain, leveraging T1195 tactics to compromise the software development lifecycle. The real-world impact of this attack includes unauthorized access to sensitive information, with potential targets including developers and organizations relying on the compromised packages. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately remove the malicious @bitwarden/cli package and update their dependencies to the latest version, ensuring that all Docker Hub, GitHub Actions, and VS Code extensions are configured to use secure and verified packages. Additionally, implementing a robust software development lifecycle security strategy, including regular package audits and secure coding practices, can help mitigate the risk of similar attacks in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1608| C[Stage Capabilities]:::tactic
    C -->|T1555.005| D[Password Managers]:::tactic
    D -->|T1104| E[Multi-Stage Channels]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Supply Chain Poisoning via PyPI Repository Compromise
items.description: Xinference, an open-source distributed AI model inference framework, suffered a supply chain attack when attackers compromised PyPI release credentials of maintainers and published three malicious versions (2.6.0, 2.6.1, 2.6.2) on April 22, 2026. The malicious code, encoded in Base64 layers within __init__.py, executes automatically upon library installation or import, collecting cloud credentials
items.ai_summary: The Xinference open-source distributed AI model inference framework suffered a supply chain attack when attackers compromised PyPI release credentials of maintainers and published three malicious versions (2.6.0, 2.6.1, 2.6.2) on April 22, 2026. The malicious code, encoded in Base64 layers within __init__.py, executes automatically upon library installation or import, collecting cloud credentials, which could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data. This attack affects users who have installed the malicious versions of the library, and the real-world impact includes potential data breaches and unauthorized access to cloud resources. To remediate, defenders should immediately uninstall the affected versions and update to a secure version, such as 2.5.9 or later, and rotate any potentially compromised cloud credentials. Additionally, developers should verify the integrity of dependencies and use tools like pip-compile to ensure reproducible builds.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1190| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1078.004| D[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    D -->|T1078.004| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A cunning predator: How Silver Fox preys on Japanese firms this tax season
items.description: Silver Fox, a threat actor, is exploiting Japan's tax filing and organizational change season with a targeted spearphishing campaign against Japanese businesses. The group sends convincing phishing emails related to tax compliance, salary adjustments, and HR matters, tricking recipients into opening malicious links or attachments. The campaign capitalizes on the high volume of legitimate financial
items.ai_summary: The Silver Fox threat actor is conducting a targeted spearphishing campaign against Japanese businesses, exploiting the country's tax filing and organizational change season. The group sends convincing phishing emails related to tax compliance, salary adjustments, and HR matters, tricking recipients into opening malicious links or attachments, utilizing the T1566 phishing tactic. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive financial information, potentially affecting multiple Japanese firms. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to detect and block phishing attempts, and educate employees on how to identify and report suspicious emails, particularly those related to tax compliance and HR matters. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a web application firewall to detect and prevent malicious link clicks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Extortion in the Enterprise: Defending Against BlackFile Attacks
items.description: Since February 2026, multiple incidents involving data theft and extortion have been attributed to activity cluster CL-CRI-1116, also known as BlackFile, UNC6671, and Cordial Spider. These financially-motivated attackers, likely associated with "The Com" collective, employ voice-based phishing combined with credential harvesting through fraudulent login pages. They impersonate IT support staff to
items.ai_summary: The BlackFile threat actor, also known as UNC6671 and Cordial Spider, has been attributed to multiple incidents of data theft and extortion since February 2026. The attackers employ voice-based phishing tactics, often impersonating IT support staff, to trick victims into divulging sensitive credentials, which are then harvested through fraudulent login pages. This initial access vector, categorized as T1566 Phishing, allows the attackers to establish a foothold within the target organization. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication, particularly for remote access and sensitive data repositories, and ensure that all employees are aware of the risks associated with voice-based phishing and know how to verify the identity of IT support staff. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious network activity, such as unusual login attempts or data exfiltration, can help detect and respond to potential BlackFile attacks, with specific attention to T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel tactics.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: 73 Open VSX Sleeper Extensions Linked to Malware Show New Activations
items.description: The GlassWorm campaign targeting Open VSX has escalated with 73 newly identified impersonation extensions. These sleeper extensions were initially published without malicious payloads by newly created GitHub accounts, appearing benign to build trust and credibility. At least six extensions have been activated to deliver malware through normal update mechanisms. The extensions clone popular legitim
items.ai_summary: The GlassWorm campaign has escalated with 73 newly identified impersonation extensions targeting Open VSX, initially published without malicious payloads to build trust and credibility. At least six extensions have been activated to deliver malware through normal update mechanisms, exploiting the trust established by their benign initial publication. The attack vector involves the manipulation of access tokens (T1134) and potentially the theft or forgery of authentication certificates (T1649), allowing the threat actors to gain unauthorized access to user accounts and systems. To remediate, defenders should immediately review and update their extension portfolios, removing any suspicious or recently activated extensions, and ensure that all GitHub accounts used for publication are thoroughly vetted. Additionally, implementing robust authentication and authorization mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication, can help mitigate the risk of token manipulation and certificate forgery.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1134| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1649| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1055| D[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    D -->|T1204| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside the Bulletproof Hosting Network Behind 16,000+ Fake Shops
items.description: Fibergrid has operated as a bulletproof hosting provider for nearly a decade, currently hosting 16,700 active fraudulent e-commerce sites. The network exploits stolen African IPv4 address space worth $20-25 million, originally acquired through improper AFRINIC registrations. Despite claiming Seychelles-based operations, multilateration analysis reveals infrastructure concentrated in the United Sta
items.ai_summary: The Fibergrid bulletproof hosting network has been operating for nearly a decade, hosting over 16,700 active fraudulent e-commerce sites. This network exploits stolen African IPv4 address space, valued at $20-25 million, which was originally acquired through improper AFRINIC registrations. The threat actors utilize virtual private servers, as indicated by TTP T1583.003, and exploit public-facing applications, as seen in TTP T1190, to facilitate their malicious activities. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust IP address filtering and monitoring, focusing on the stolen African IPv4 address space, and ensure that all public-facing applications are up-to-date and securely configured. Additionally, defenders should conduct regular multilateration analysis to identify and track suspicious infrastructure, such as that concentrated in the United States in this case.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.003| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1190| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: OT-Focused Malware Highlights Emerging Risk to Water Infrastructure Systems
items.description: ZionSiphon is operational technology-focused malware targeting water treatment and desalination facilities in Israel. The sample demonstrates ICS-awareness through industrial protocol interaction capabilities including Modbus, with incomplete support for DNP3 and S7comm. It incorporates geographic and environmental validation controls designed to restrict execution to Israeli water infrastructure
items.ai_summary: The ZionSiphon malware targets water treatment and desalination facilities in Israel, demonstrating industrial control system (ICS) awareness through its interaction capabilities with industrial protocols such as Modbus. The malware has incomplete support for DNP3 and S7comm protocols, indicating a potential for expansion to other systems. If exploited, this could lead to significant disruptions in water supply, affecting thousands of people. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network segmentation, restrict access to ICS systems, and monitor for unusual Modbus traffic. Additionally, updating to the latest versions of ICS software and implementing a robust security information and event management (SIEM) system can help detect and respond to potential threats.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A Third Vultr Seoul Box: 60+ Kimsuky Domains, 18 Months of DDNS Rotation, and a 5-Year Infrastructure Trail
items.description: This analysis documents a third Vultr Seoul VPS (158.247.210.58) associated with Kimsuky operations, featuring over 60 domains across an 18-month period of systematic credential harvesting infrastructure. The actor demonstrates deliberate rotation through seven DDNS providers to evade blocklisting while maintaining the same backend VPS since at least September 2020. The domains systematically impe
items.ai_summary: The Kimsuky threat actor has been utilizing a third Vultr Seoul VPS, with the IP address 158.247.210.58, to conduct credential harvesting operations. Over 60 domains have been rotated through seven DDNS providers over an 18-month period to evade blocklisting, while maintaining the same backend VPS since at least September 2020. This deliberate rotation allows the actor to continuously conduct phishing and credential harvesting campaigns, targeting various organizations and individuals. To remediate, defenders should implement DNS traffic monitoring to detect and block suspicious DDNS activity, and update their blocklists to include the identified domains and IP addresses. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular security audits to identify and mitigate potential vulnerabilities in their infrastructure.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.003| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1189| C[Credential Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Tall Tales: How Chinese Actors Use Impersonation and Stolen Narratives to Perpetuate Digital Transnational Repression
items.description: In collaboration with the International Consortium of Investigative Journalists (ICIJ), two distinct actor clusters aligned with the People's Republic of China were identified targeting journalists and civil society members. GLITTER CARP conducted widespread credential harvesting campaigns against Uyghur, Tibetan, Taiwanese, and Hong Kong diaspora activists, as well as journalists covering these c
items.ai_summary: This threat involves Chinese actors impersonating individuals and using stolen narratives to target journalists and civil society members. The actors, identified as GLITTER CARP, conducted credential harvesting campaigns against Uyghur, Tibetan, Taiwanese, and Hong Kong diaspora activists, as well as journalists covering these communities. The attackers used T1134 (Access Token Manipulation) to gain unauthorized access to the victims' accounts. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication, monitor account activity for suspicious behavior, and educate users on the dangers of phishing and social engineering attacks. Additionally, organizations should consider implementing security awareness training programs to help employees recognize and report suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1134| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-6568: A vulnerability was determined in kodcloud KodExplorer up to 4.52. This affects ...
items.description: A vulnerability was determined in kodcloud KodExplorer up to 4.52. This affects the function share.class.php::initShareOld of the file /app/controller/share.class.php of the component Public Share Handler. This manipulation of the argument path causes path traversal. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacted early ab
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6568 is a path traversal vulnerability in kodcloud KodExplorer up to version 4.52. The vulnerability affects the function share.class.php::initShareOld in the /app/controller/share.class.php file of the Public Share Handler component. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the path argument remotely, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data disclosure. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to version 4.53 or later, and ensure that the 'allow_url_include' PHP configuration option is set to 'Off'. Additionally, it is recommended to restrict access to the Public Share Handler component to trusted users only.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-6564: A vulnerability was found in EMQ EMQX Enterprise up to 6.1.0. The impacted eleme...
items.description: A vulnerability was found in EMQ EMQX Enterprise up to 6.1.0. The impacted element is an unknown function of the component Session Handling. The manipulation results in improper authorization. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6564 is a vulnerability in EMQ EMQX Enterprise up to version 6.1.0, specifically affecting the Session Handling component. The issue allows for improper authorization, enabling remote attacks. The vulnerability has been publicly disclosed and could be exploited by threat actors. To remediate, users should update to version 6.2.0 or later, and ensure that the Session Handling component is properly configured. Additionally, it is recommended to implement strict access controls and monitor system logs for suspicious activity.

items.title: CVE-2026-6563: A vulnerability has been found in H3C Magic B1 up to 100R004. The affected eleme...
items.description: A vulnerability has been found in H3C Magic B1 up to 100R004. The affected element is the function SetAPWifiorLedInfoById of the file /goform/aspForm. The manipulation of the argument param leads to buffer overflow. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond i
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6563 is a buffer overflow vulnerability in the H3C Magic B1 up to version 100R004, specifically in the SetAPWifiorLedInfoById function of the /goform/aspForm file. An attacker can remotely exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the param argument, leading to client execution. If exploited, this vulnerability can result in arbitrary code execution, allowing the attacker to gain control of the affected system. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to version 100R005 or later, and ensure that the SetAPWifiorLedInfoById function is properly validated to prevent buffer overflow attacks. Additionally, users should consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent such attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-6562: A flaw has been found in dameng100 muucmf 1.9.5.20260309. Impacted is the functi...
items.description: A flaw has been found in dameng100 muucmf 1.9.5.20260309. Impacted is the function getListByPage of the file /index/Search/index.html. Executing a manipulation of the argument keyword can lead to sql injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been published and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6562 is a remote SQL injection vulnerability in dameng100 muucmf 1.9.5.20260309, allowing attackers to manipulate the 'keyword' argument in the 'getListByPage' function of /index/Search/index.html. This flaw can be exploited by executing a crafted input, potentially leading to unauthorized data access or even full control of the affected system. The vulnerability has been publicly disclosed and may be used by threat actors, posing a significant risk to systems running the affected version. To mitigate this issue, administrators should update to dameng100 muucmf 1.9.6 or later, ensuring the 'getListByPage' function is properly sanitized. Additionally, implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) can help detect and prevent such attacks.

items.description: A vulnerability was detected in EyouCMS up to 1.7.1. This issue affects the function edit_adminlogo of the file application/admin/controller/Index.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument filename results in unrestricted upload. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respon
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6561 is a remote file upload vulnerability in EyouCMS versions up to 1.7.1. The issue affects the edit_adminlogo function in application/admin/controller/Index.php, allowing attackers to upload files without restrictions. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to arbitrary file execution, potentially allowing attackers to gain control of the affected system. To remediate, update to EyouCMS version 1.7.2 or later, and ensure the application/admin/controller/Index.php file is modified to validate the filename argument. Additionally, consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-41242: protobufjs compiles protobuf definitions into JavaScript (JS) functions. In vers...
items.description: protobufjs compiles protobuf definitions into JavaScript (JS) functions. In versions prior to 8.0.1 and 7.5.5, attackers can inject arbitrary code in the "type" fields of protobuf definitions, which will then execute during object decoding using that definition. Versions 8.0.1 and 7.5.5 patch the issue.

items.description: Agentic AI can be expensive to use, causing further and unpredictable pressure on tight budgets. The post Sevii Launches Cyber Swarm Defense to Make Agentic AI Security Costs Predictable appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
items.description: Every security program is betting on the same assumption: once a system is connected, the problem is solved. Open a ticket, stand up a gateway, push the data through. Done. That assumption is wrong. It is also a major reason Zero Trust programs stall. New research my team just published puts numbers on it. The Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace report, based on a survey of 500 security

items.title: Electric Motorcycles and Scooters Face Hacking Risks to Security and Rider Safety
items.description: Vulnerabilities in Zero Motorcycles electric motorcycles and Yadea electric scooters can pose physical security and safety risks. The post Electric Motorcycles and Scooters Face Hacking Risks to Security and Rider Safety appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: No Patch for New PhantomRPC Privilege Escalation Technique in Windows
items.description: A fake RPC server can be used to listen for RPC requests and impersonate the target service to elevate privileges to System. The post No Patch for New PhantomRPC Privilege Escalation Technique in Windows appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Unpatched Flaw Leaves Hugging Face LeRobot Open to Unauthenticated RCE
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security flaw impacting LeRobot, Hugging Face's open-source robotics platform with nearly 24,000 GitHub stars, that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-25874 (CVSS score: 9.3), which has been described as a case of untrusted data deserialization stemming from the use of the

items.title: Germany Suspects Russia Is Behind Signal Phishing That Targeted Top Officials
items.description: Federal prosecutors have been conducting a preliminary investigation since mid-February 2026 into alleged cyberattacks on Signal accounts. The post Germany Suspects Russia Is Behind Signal Phishing That Targeted Top Officials appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: After Mythos: New Playbooks For a Zero-Window Era
items.description: When patching isn’t fast enough, NDR helps contain the next era of threats. If you’ve been tracking advancements in AI, you know the exploit window, the short buffer that organizations relied on to patch and protect after a vulnerability disclosure, is closing fast. Anthropic’s new model, Claude Mythos, and its Project Glasswing, showed that finding exploitable vulnerabilities and subtle cracks

items.title: Microsoft: New Remote Desktop warnings may display incorrectly
items.description: Microsoft has confirmed a new issue causing newly introduced Windows security warnings to display incorrectly when opening Remote Desktop (.rdp) files. [...]

items.title: Microsoft asks iPhone users to reauthenticate after Outlook outage
items.description: After addressing a widespread outage that affected Outlook.com users worldwide on Monday, Microsoft has asked iPhone users to re-enter their credentials to regain access to their Outlook and Hotmail accounts via the default Mail app. [...]

items.title: Spectrum Security Emerges From Stealth Mode With $19 Million
items.description: The threat detection startup will invest in accelerating its engineering and go-to-market efforts. The post Spectrum Security Emerges From Stealth Mode With $19 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chinese Silk Typhoon Hacker Extradited to U.S. Over COVID Research Cyberattacks
items.description: A Chinese national accused of being a member of the Silk Typhoon hacking group has been extradited to the U.S. from Italy.  Xu Zewei, 34, was arrested in July 2025 by Italian authorities for his alleged links to the Chinese state-sponsored threat group and for orchestrating cyber attacks against American organizations and government agencies between February 2020 and June 2021, including

items.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
items.description: An administrative role meant for artificial intelligence (AI) agents within Microsoft Entra ID could enable privilege escalation and identity takeover attacks, according to new findings from Silverfort. Agent ID Administrator is a privileged built-in role introduced by Microsoft as part of its agent identity platform to handle all aspects of an AI agent's identity lifecycle operations in a

items.title: Medtronic Hack Confirmed After ShinyHunters Threatens Data Leak
items.description: The ShinyHunters cybercrime group claimed to have stolen 9 million records containing personal information from Medtronic. The post Medtronic Hack Confirmed After ShinyHunters Threatens Data Leak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
items.description: Microsoft on Monday revised its advisory for a now-patched, high-severity security flaw impacting Windows Shell to acknowledge that it has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-32202 (CVSS score: 4.3), a spoofing vulnerability that could allow an attacker to access sensitive information. It was addressed as part of its Patch Tuesday update for this

items.title: Robinhood account creation flaw abused to send phishing emails
items.description: Online trading platform Robinhood's account creation process was exploited by threat actors to inject phishing messages into legitimate emails, tricking users into believing their accounts had suspicious activity. [...]

items.title: GlassWorm malware attacks return via 73 OpenVSX "sleeper" extensions
items.description: A new wave of the Glassworm campaign is targeting the OpenVSX ecosystem with 73 "sleeper" extensions that turn malicious after an update. [...]

items.title: Supreme Court signals location data searches should require a warrant
items.description: Privacy advocates had worried that the high court would rule that geofencing does not qualify as a constitutionally protected search, opening the door to much broader use of warrantless reverse searches of all types.

items.title: UNC6692 Combines Social Engineering, Malware, Cloud Abuse
items.description: A newly discovered threat actor is using Microsoft Teams, AWS S3 buckets, and custom "Snow" malware in a multipronged campaign.

items.title: Tennessee becomes second state to ban cryptocurrency ATMs over scam concerns
items.description: State officials said they observed overseas criminals carrying out government impersonation or tech support cons, as well as romance and pig butchering scams using cryptocurrency ATMs.

items.title: Canada arrests three for operating “SMS blaster” device in Toronto
items.description: Canadian authorities have arrested three men for operating an "SMS blaster" device that pretends to be a cellular tower to send phishing texts to nearby phones. [...]

items.title: Alleged Silk Typhoon hacker extradited to US for cyberespionage
items.description: A Chinese national accused of carrying out cyberespionage operations for China's intelligence services has been extradited from Italy to the United States to face criminal charges. [...]

items.title: Money launderer for crypto thieves given 5-year sentence
items.description: A California man was sentenced to more than five years in prison for his role in supporting a cybercriminal organization that stole about $260 million worth of cryptocurrency from victims.

items.title: FTC: Americans lost over $2.1 billion to social media scams in 2025
items.description: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) warned of a massive increase in losses from social media scams since 2020, exceeding $2.1 billion in 2025. [...]

items.title: Unpatched 'PhantomRPC' Flaw in Windows Enables Privilege Escalation
items.description: A researcher discovered five different exploit paths that stem from an architectural weakness in how Windows' Remote Procedure Call (RPC) mechanism handles connections to unavailable services.

items.title: Disinformation campaign targeted Tibetan parliament-in-exile elections
items.description: The operation, identified by the Digital Forensic Research Lab (DFRLab), was part of Spamouflage, a long-running influence network linked to Beijing.

items.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
items.description: Checkmarx has disclosed that its ongoing investigation tied to the supply chain security incident has revealed that a cybercriminal group published data related to the company on the dark web. "Based on current evidence, we believe this data originated from Checkmarx's GitHub repository, and that access to that repository was facilitated through the initial supply chain attack of March 23, 2026,

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 008 - 26-Day Pause Ends with Three Concurrent Compromises (Checkmarx KICS, Bitwarden CLI Cascade, xinference PyPI), CanisterSprawl npm Worm Identified, and Tier 1 Coverage Returns, (Mon, Apr 27th)
items.description: This update succeeds&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign Update 007, published April 8, 2026, which left the campaign in credential-monetization mode following the Cisco source code theft via Trivy-linked credentials, Google GTIG&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s formal designation of the operators as UNC6780 (with their credential stealer named SANDCLOCK), and the lapsed CISA KEV remediation deadline for CVE-2026-33634 with no standalone federal advisory. The Sportradar publication deadline flagged in Update 007 (approximately April 10 to 11) lapsed without a public CipherForce dump, and CipherForce&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s leak infrastructure has remained offline. Twelve days after Update 007, the technical compromise picture changed sharply across the W17 window (April 20 through April 26).

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
items.description: Everything is dumb again. This week feels broken in a very familiar way. Old tricks are back. New tools are doing shady crap. Supply chains got hit. Fake help desks worked. Weird research showed how easy some attacks still are. Most of it feels like stuff we should have fixed years ago. Bad extensions. Stolen creds. Remote tools are getting abused. Malware hides in places people trust. Same

items.title: 20-Year-Old Malware Rewrites History of Cyber Sabotage
items.description: Researchers have uncovered a malware framework dubbed "fast16" that predates Stuxnet by five years.

items.title: Incomplete Windows Patch Opens Door to Zero-Click Attacks
items.description: The initial vulnerability was exploited by Russia-linked APT28 in attacks against Ukraine and EU countries. The post Incomplete Windows Patch Opens Door to Zero-Click Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Parsing Agentic Offensive Security's Existential Threat
items.description: Some fear frontier LLMs like Claude Mythos and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 will lead to cybersecurity annihilation. Ari Herbert-Voss notes this could be an opportunity.

items.title: Mythos Changed the Math on Vulnerability Discovery. Most Teams Aren't Ready for the Remediation Side
items.description: Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Preview has dominated security discussions since its April 7 announcement. Early reporting describes a powerful cybersecurity-focused AI system capable of identifying vulnerabilities at scale and raising serious questions about how quickly organizations can validate, prioritize, and remediate what it finds. The debate that followed has mostly focused on the right

items.title: Helping Romance Scam Victims Requires a Proactive, Empathic Approach
items.description: People targeted by confidence schemes find getting help is a lonely road. Experts want law enforcement, financial, and government institutions to work together and protect them.

items.title: US Busts Myanmar Ring Targeting US Citizens in Financial Fraud
items.description: Some 29 people were charged, including a Cambodian senator, and authorities seized more than 500 Web domains tied to fake investment sites.

items.title: Glasswing Secured the Code. The Rest of Your Stack Is Still on You
items.description: Forgotten integrations, shadow IT, SaaS, and now shadow AI and agents are everywhere, and attackers don't need sophisticated AI models to take advantage.

items.title: AI Phishing Is No. 1 With a Bullet for Cyberattackers
items.description: In the past six months, companies have seen a significant influx of AI-powered phishing, as cyberattackers progress from small campaigns to 1-to-1 personalized attacks.

items.title: North Korea's Lazarus Targets macOS Users via ClickFix
items.description: Lazarus continues leveraging ClickFix for initial access and data theft: in this case, against Mac-centric organizations and their high-value leaders.

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.description: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, April 21st, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9900, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
CISA Releases Product Categories List to Propel Post-Quantum Cryptography Adoption Pursuant to President Trump’s Executive Order 14306
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
